inclusion_criteria: Direct evidence must involve synthetic, simulated, generated, mismatched, or augmented student/learner responses used in automated scoring, scorer development, detection, validation, or assessment-feedback workflows.
Priority sources must address summative, high-stakes, grade-bearing, operational, formative, in-term, or classroom consequences rather than only no-consequence simulation quality.
Operational reports and primary peer-reviewed or conference papers are preferred over secondary commentary.
Full text or official source pages must be available for extraction.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude automated scoring studies without synthetic or constructed response sets in the development or evaluation workflow.
Exclude generic AI assessment policy unless it directly mentions synthetic responses or is needed as an assessment-stakes boundary condition.
Exclude sources that only evaluate LLM scoring of real responses without generated, simulated, or synthetic response artifacts.
Do not treat off-topic detection or stress testing as final automated-score validation.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by canonical title, DOI, official report URL, source page, and existing source_id.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 35 records across targeted web, official pages, and existing graph audit

screening_decisions.title: 2025 New Mexico MSSA Technical Report
screening_decisions.reason: Direct official operational state summative assessment evidence for LLM-generated synthetic responses as limited automated-scoring training augmentation.

screening_decisions.title: Automatic Detection of Off-Topic Spoken Responses Using Very Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
screening_decisions.reason: Adjacent high-stakes spoken assessment evidence for synthetic/mismatched responses used in validity-protection detection rather than scoring validation.

screening_decisions.title: Automated Scoring of Communication Skills in Physician-Patient Interaction: Balancing Performance and Scalability
screening_decisions.reason: Direct formative/professional-education evidence for synthetic learner responses as training augmentation with real learner-response evaluation and summative human-review caveats.

screening_decisions.title: Exploring Data Augmentation and Resampling Strategies for Transformer-Based Models to Address Class Imbalance in AI Scoring of Scientific Explanations in NGSS Classroom
screening_decisions.reason: Direct classroom/in-term evidence for GPT-generated synthetic responses as training augmentation with held-out real-response evaluation and explicit validity-work limitations.

screening_decisions.title: NM-MSSA New Mexico Help & Support resources page
screening_decisions.reason: Used as official locator for the technical report rather than separate direct evidence.

screening_decisions.title: Evaluating LLMs for Automated Scoring in Formative Assessments
screening_decisions.reason: Relevant to lower-stakes LLM automated scoring but not synthetic student responses as a development or validation corpus.

screening_decisions.title: A Multilingual Dataset of Student Answers, Human Grading, and Multi-LLM Evaluations for Automated Assessment Research Using JorGPT
screening_decisions.reason: Automated assessment benchmark with authentic student answers, not synthetic student responses.

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging Knowledge Profiles and Generative AI for Realistic Student Response Generation
screening_decisions.reason: Potentially relevant to in-term automated-grading augmentation but already marked as access/scope follow-up in the previous baseline pass.

screening_summary: This targeted update searched explicitly for consequential assessment contexts. The strongest new evidence is the New Mexico MSSA technical report, which changes the prior high-stakes boundary from mostly adjacent/diagnostic evidence to direct but tightly controlled operational training-augmentation evidence. The other included sources clarify high-stakes detection/stress-testing, formative professional education, and classroom/in-term augmentation boundaries.
limitations: This is a targeted review-update pass, not a systematic review.
The search focused on English-language accessible full texts and official reports.
The New Mexico report is a single operational state-assessment source and should not be generalized into a universal high-stakes policy.
The included lower-stakes studies support training augmentation and feedback-oriented development, not synthetic-only validation or grade-bearing deployment.
